title: Add field indexer for Secret-to-Keystone mapper to reduce AP
description:
# GitHub Issue #194: Add field indexer for Secret-to-Keystone mapper to reduce API server load

**Category**: performance | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: Replace the current secretToKeystoneMapper implementation (keystone_controller.go:213) which lists all Keystone CRs on every Secret event with a field indexer registered via mgr.GetFieldIndexer(). Index Keystone CRs by their referenced Secret names so the mapper can perform an O(1) indexed lookup instead of an O(n) list operation.

**Motivation**: In namespaces with many Secrets (common with ExternalSecretOperator), every Secret create/update/delete triggers a full list of all Keystone CRs against the API server. This generates unnecessary API server load that scales linearly with Secret count. Field indexers are the standard controller-runtime pattern for efficient reverse lookups.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Field indexer registered for Secret name references on Keystone CRs
- [ ] secretToKeystoneMapper uses indexed lookup instead of List
- [ ] API server request count for Keystone CR listing reduced to zero during Secret-only events
- [ ] Unit test verifies mapper returns correct reconcile requests via index
- [ ] No behavioral change in reconcile triggering

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/194

stories.title: Cluster operator sees reduced API server load from Secret events
stories.want: the Keystone controller to avoid listing all Keystone CRs on every Secret event
stories.so_that: namespaces with many ESO-managed Secrets do not generate linearly scaling load on the kube-apiserver
criteria: Secret create/update/delete events in a Keystone namespace do NOT trigger a LIST on Keystone CRs
The controller registers a field indexer via mgr.GetFieldIndexer() during SetupWithManager (CC-0087)
The indexer covers both spec.database.secretRef.name and spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef.name
An integration test confirms that when a Secret event arrives, only an indexed cache lookup is performed (no List RPC)

stories.title: Keystone operator developer retains existing reconcile-trigger behavior
stories.want: the refactored mapper to enqueue reconcile requests for every Secret that previously did
stories.so_that: no behavioral regression is introduced when replacing List with an indexed lookup (CC-0087)
criteria: A Secret whose name matches spec.database.secretRef.name enqueues exactly one reconcile request for the referencing Keystone CR
A Secret whose name matches spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef.name enqueues exactly one reconcile request for the referencing Keystone CR
A Secret that is owner-referenced to a Keystone CR (e.g. rotation staging Secret) still enqueues a reconcile request
A Secret unrelated to any Keystone CR enqueues zero requests
Matching is namespace-scoped: Secrets in namespace A never enqueue Keystone CRs from namespace B

stories.title: Keystone operator developer finds the indexer setup documented
stories.want: the field indexer and mapper flow documented in the reconciler reference
stories.so_that: future changes to Secret references add new indexed fields correctly (CC-0087)
criteria: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md describes the field indexer: key, indexed fields, registration site
The doc explicitly lists which Secret-referencing fields on Keystone contribute to the index
The doc records the fallback path for owner-referenced Secrets (rotation staging Secrets) that are not reachable via the indexed fields
The doc links to the mapper function name and file path

stories.title: Reviewer confirms no behavioral regression in end-to-end reconcile triggering
stories.want: unit and integration tests to exercise the indexed mapper end-to-end against envtest
stories.so_that: I can sign off on CC-0087 with confidence that no Secret event is silently dropped
criteria: Unit tests cover referenced Secret name, owner-referenced Secret, unrelated Secret, and cross-namespace Secret cases
An envtest integration test registers the indexer, creates a Keystone CR, mutates a referenced Secret, and verifies reconcile is enqueued exactly once
Tests for the existing TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_* suite continue to pass with the refactored implementation
No test asserts List being called; the test pins indexed-lookup behavior without coupling to a specific RPC count

stories.title: Cluster operator diagnoses a broken indexer without silent reconcile failures
stories.want: the controller to fail startup loudly when the indexer cannot be registered
stories.so_that: a silently unindexed mapper does not pass review unnoticed (CC-0087)
criteria: SetupWithManager returns the error from GetFieldIndexer().IndexField(...) so manager startup aborts
The error message names the index key so the operator can diagnose from logs alone
An envtest test confirms that a successful manager start implies the indexer is registered

requirements.description: The Keystone controller SHALL register a field indexer on Keystone CRs that indexes the union of referenced Secret names (spec.database.secretRef.name and spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef.name) under a single, stable index key (CC-0087).
requirements.rationale: A single index key keyed by Secret name enables O(1) reverse lookup for the two fields that drive reconcile from Secret events, which is the primary performance goal of CC-0087.

scenarios.name: Indexer registered at manager startup
scenarios.when: SetupWithManager is called with a controller manager
scenarios.then: mgr.GetFieldIndexer().IndexField is invoked exactly once for &keystonev1alpha1.Keystone{} with the documented index key
and_then: The extractor returns both ks.Spec.Database.SecretRef.Name and ks.Spec.Bootstrap.AdminPasswordSecretRef.Name (deduplicated if equal)
The extractor filters out empty strings so they do not pollute the index

scenarios.name: Indexer registration failure aborts startup
scenarios.when: GetFieldIndexer().IndexField returns an error (e.g. duplicate key, scheme missing)
scenarios.then: SetupWithManager returns that error wrapped with the index key context
and_then: The manager does not start the controller
The wrapped error identifies the offending index key for debugging (CC-0087)

scenarios.name: Extractor handles both fields independently
scenarios.when: A Keystone CR has spec.database.secretRef.name="a" and spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef.name="b"
scenarios.then: The indexer produces two entries for that CR: one under "a" and one under "b"
and_then: A lookup under either name returns the same Keystone CR

requirements.description: The secretToKeystoneMapper SHALL perform a namespaced List with client.MatchingFields against the registered index to find Keystone CRs referencing the incoming Secret by name, replacing the prior unfiltered List (CC-0087).
requirements.rationale: Replacing the unfiltered per-namespace List with an indexed cache lookup is the core performance improvement specified in the issue (#194).

scenarios.name: Indexed lookup returns matching Keystone
scenarios.when: A Secret named "keystone-db" is observed in namespace "default" and a Keystone CR in that namespace references it via spec.database.secretRef.name
scenarios.then: The mapper calls c.List with client.InNamespace("default") and client.MatchingFields{indexKey: "keystone-db"}
and_then: The result slice contains exactly one reconcile.Request pointing at that Keystone CR
No unfiltered List call is issued

scenarios.name: Indexed lookup returns no match
scenarios.when: A Secret named "unrelated" is observed and no Keystone CR indexes "unrelated"
scenarios.then: The mapper returns a nil or empty slice from the indexed lookup
and_then: No reconcile.Request is enqueued from the indexed path

scenarios.name: Lookup is namespace-scoped
scenarios.when: A Secret is observed in namespace A and a matching Keystone CR exists only in namespace B
scenarios.then: The mapper returns an empty slice
and_then: Cross-namespace Secret events never trigger reconcile

scenarios.name: Indexed lookup error is logged and swallowed
scenarios.when: c.List returns an error
scenarios.then: The mapper logs the error with context and returns nil
and_then: The controller-runtime queue is not poisoned by the error
Behavior mirrors the current error handling on List failure

requirements.description: The secretToKeystoneMapper SHALL additionally enqueue any Keystone CRs referenced by the incoming Secret's ownerReferences (Kind=Keystone, matching apiVersion), using a direct per-ref lookup rather than a List (CC-0087, CC-0081).
requirements.rationale: Rotation staging Secrets and other owner-referenced Secrets are not reachable through the secret-name index; preserving this enqueue path is required to avoid regressing CC-0081.

scenarios.name: Owned Secret still enqueues owner
scenarios.when: A Secret carries an ownerReference with UID matching an existing Keystone CR and Kind=Keystone
scenarios.then: The mapper enqueues a reconcile.Request for that Keystone CR derived from ownerRef.Name + Secret.Namespace
and_then: The enqueue happens without calling List on Keystone CRs
No duplicate request is produced if the same Keystone is also reached via the indexed path

scenarios.name: Non-Keystone owner ref ignored
scenarios.when: A Secret carries an ownerReference with Kind!=Keystone or apiVersion from a different group
scenarios.then: The mapper ignores that owner reference
and_then: No reconcile.Request is enqueued from that owner ref

scenarios.name: Orphan labeled staging Secret is not enqueued
scenarios.when: A Secret carries the StagingSecretLabelKey but has no ownerReferences and no matching referenced name
scenarios.then: The mapper returns an empty slice

requirements.description: The refactor SHALL NOT change the observable set of reconcile.Request values emitted by the mapper for any Secret input already covered by existing unit tests (CC-0087).
requirements.rationale: The issue mandates no behavioral change in reconcile triggering; parity with current tests is the operational definition of that constraint.

scenarios.name: Referenced Secret parity
scenarios.when: TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_ReferencedSecrets runs against the refactored mapper
scenarios.then: All assertions pass unchanged

scenarios.name: Owned Secret parity
scenarios.when: TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_OwnedSecrets runs against the refactored mapper
scenarios.then: All assertions pass unchanged

scenarios.name: Staging Secret parity
scenarios.when: TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_EnqueuesOnStagingSecretUpdate and TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_OrphanStagingSecretNotEnqueued run against the refactored mapper
scenarios.then: All assertions pass unchanged

requirements.description: The mapper SHALL deduplicate reconcile.Request values so a single Secret event never enqueues the same Keystone CR twice when it is reachable via both the indexed field path and an owner reference (CC-0087).
requirements.rationale: Duplicate enqueues waste work-queue slots and can inflate reconcile metrics; the prior implementation implicitly deduplicated via a single iteration over KeystoneList.

scenarios.name: Referenced and owner-referenced same CR
scenarios.when: A Secret is both named like spec.database.secretRef.name AND owner-referenced to the same Keystone CR
scenarios.then: The returned slice contains exactly one reconcile.Request for that CR

scenarios.name: Two distinct Keystones, one Secret
scenarios.when: A Secret named "shared" is referenced by two Keystone CRs in the same namespace
scenarios.then: The returned slice contains exactly two reconcile.Requests, one per CR

requirements.description: The indexer key name SHALL be exported or declared as a package-level const so tests and documentation can reference the exact string used at registration and lookup sites (CC-0087).
requirements.rationale: Using a literal string in two places (register and lookup) risks silent drift; a single named constant keeps the two sites in sync and makes the key discoverable in docs.

scenarios.name: Single constant used at both sites
scenarios.when: The codebase is grepped for the index key string
scenarios.then: Exactly one declaration exists, referenced by both IndexField and the MatchingFields lookup
and_then: Test files import the same constant rather than redeclaring the string

scenarios.name: Key is documented
scenarios.when: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md is read
scenarios.then: The index key name and indexed fields are listed verbatim

requirements.description: The mapper SHOULD remain O(k) in the number of matching Keystone CRs k rather than O(n) in total Keystone CRs in the namespace (CC-0087).
requirements.rationale: This encodes the performance improvement objective numerically; it is testable via inspection of the mapper body (no unfiltered List).

scenarios.name: No unfiltered list calls
scenarios.when: The source of secretToKeystoneMapper is inspected
scenarios.then: It contains no c.List call without client.MatchingFields or client.ObjectKey lookup

scenarios.name: Owner path avoids list
scenarios.when: The owner-ref path is exercised
scenarios.then: Only per-ref name construction is performed; no List is issued

requirements.description: The integration test SHALL register the field indexer via the manager's GetFieldIndexer in setupEnvTestWithController so that the integration path mirrors production (CC-0087).
requirements.rationale: Divergence between production SetupWithManager and the test's ad-hoc controller wiring would allow a missing indexer to pass CI; mirroring the registration closes that gap.

scenarios.name: Integration setup registers indexer
scenarios.when: setupEnvTestWithController builds the controller
scenarios.then: It calls the same helper function used by production SetupWithManager to register the indexer
and_then: If that helper returns an error, the test fails fast

scenarios.name: Integration test Secret event triggers reconcile
scenarios.when: A Keystone CR references Secret "keystone-db" and that Secret is created
scenarios.then: The Keystone is reconciled within eventuallyTimeout

tasks.title: Declare exported const KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey and a package-level extractor func keystoneSecretNameExtractor(obj client.Object) []string in keystone_controller.go; extractor returns deduplicated, non-empty Secret names from spec.database.secretRef.name and spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef.name (REQ-001, REQ-006, CC-0087)

Write unit tests TestKeystoneSecretIndexer_ExtractsBothReferencedSecretNames, TestKeystoneSecretIndexer_DeduplicatesIdenticalNames, TestKeystoneSecretIndexer_SkipsEmptyNames in keystone_controller_test.go verifying the extractor contract (REQ-001, CC-0087)

Add registerSecretNameIndex(ctx context.Context, indexer client.FieldIndexer) error helper in keystone_controller.go that calls indexer.IndexField(ctx, &keystonev1alpha1.Keystone{}, KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey, keystoneSecretNameExtractor) and wraps errors with the index key; wire it into SetupWithManager before the Watches chain, returning any error (REQ-001, REQ-006, CC-0087)

Refactor secretToKeystoneMapper to: (a) perform a namespace-scoped List with client.MatchingFields{KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey: obj.GetName()}; (b) iterate obj.GetOwnerReferences() for Kind=Keystone and apiVersion matching keystonev1alpha1.GroupVersion.String() and enqueue by ownerRef.Name; (c) deduplicate resulting reconcile.Request values by types.NamespacedName; (d) log and swallow List errors identically to the current implementation; remove the old KeystoneList iteration and the existing isOwnedBy helper if it becomes unused (REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-005, REQ-007, CC-0087)

Write unit tests TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_UsesIndexedLookup, TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_IndexedLookupScopedToNamespace, TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_IndexedLookupErrorLoggedAndSwallowed, TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_NoUnfilteredListCall in keystone_controller_test.go using fake.NewClientBuilder().WithIndex(&keystonev1alpha1.Keystone{}, KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey, keystoneSecretNameExtractor) and interceptor.Funcs to observe List call shapes (REQ-002, REQ-007, CC-0087)

Write unit tests TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_OwnerRefPathDoesNotList and TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_DeduplicatesIndexAndOwnerPaths and TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_MultipleReferencingKeystonesAllEnqueued in keystone_controller_test.go, pinning the owner-ref enqueue path and the deduplication invariant (REQ-003, REQ-005, CC-0087)

Update existing tests TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_ReferencedSecrets, TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_OwnedSecrets, TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_EnqueuesOnStagingSecretUpdate, TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_OrphanStagingSecretNotEnqueued to build the fake client with WithIndex so the refactored mapper has an index to query, and assert they still pass without behavioral change (REQ-004, CC-0087)
Add unit test TestKeystoneSecretIndexerKey_IsDeclaredAsConst asserting the exported const exists and matches the string used at registration; prevents silent drift to a literal (REQ-006, CC-0087)

Update operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go:setupEnvTestWithController to invoke registerSecretNameIndex(ctx, mgr.GetFieldIndexer()) before Complete(r), returning any error so the test fails fast on registration failure (REQ-008, CC-0087)

Add integration test TestIntegration_SecretEventTriggersReconcileViaIndexer in integration_test.go: create Keystone referencing Secret "keystone-db", create that Secret, then Eventually assert the Keystone's ObservedGeneration or status condition updates within eventuallyTimeout (REQ-008, CC-0087)

Add integration test TestIntegration_UnrelatedSecretDoesNotTriggerReconcile in integration_test.go: snapshot Keystone ResourceVersion, create an unrelated Secret in the same namespace, then Consistently assert no new reconcile-driven status change occurs within a short window (REQ-008, CC-0087)

Add integration test TestIntegration_IndexerRegistrationFailsManagerStartCleanly in integration_test.go: call registerSecretNameIndex twice with the same key against the manager's FieldIndexer and assert the second call returns an error whose message mentions KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey (REQ-001, CC-0087)

Update docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md: in the Watches section, document the new field indexer — index key name (KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey), indexed fields (spec.database.secretRef.name, spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef.name), registration site (SetupWithManager), lookup site (secretToKeystoneMapper), and the owner-ref fallback path for rotation staging Secrets; add a short note explaining the API-server-load motivation and link back to CC-0087 (REQ-006, CC-0087)

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator sees reduced API server load from Secret events
test_specifications.expected: [unit] The indexer extractor returns a slice containing both spec.database.secretRef.name and spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef.name for a Keystone CR with distinct values

test_specifications.story: Keystone operator developer retains existing reconcile-trigger behavior
test_specifications.expected: [unit] When both referenced fields hold the same name, the extractor returns that name only once (no duplicate index entries)

test_specifications.story: Keystone operator developer retains existing reconcile-trigger behavior
test_specifications.expected: [unit] The extractor omits empty strings so unset fields do not pollute the index

test_specifications.story: Keystone operator developer retains existing reconcile-trigger behavior
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Existing test continues to pass after refactor: Secret matching spec.database.secretRef.name or spec.bootstrap.adminPasswordSecretRef.name enqueues the referencing Keystone

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator sees reduced API server load from Secret events
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Using a fake client built with WithIndex for the Keystone indexed field, the mapper returns the correct Keystone without performing any unfiltered List (assert via interceptor counting List calls with no field selector)

test_specifications.story: Keystone operator developer retains existing reconcile-trigger behavior
test_specifications.expected: [unit] A Secret in namespace A does not enqueue a matching Keystone in namespace B even when both reference the same Secret name

test_specifications.story: Reviewer confirms no behavioral regression in end-to-end reconcile triggering
test_specifications.expected: [unit] When the indexed List returns an error, the mapper logs it and returns nil rather than panicking or returning an error

test_specifications.story: Keystone operator developer retains existing reconcile-trigger behavior
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Existing test continues to pass: a Secret carrying an ownerReference matching the Keystone UID enqueues a reconcile for that Keystone

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator sees reduced API server load from Secret events
test_specifications.expected: [unit] The owner-ref enqueue path does not invoke c.List at all; only ownerRef iteration occurs (assert via interceptor)

test_specifications.story: Keystone operator developer retains existing reconcile-trigger behavior
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Existing test continues to pass: rotation staging Secrets owned by the Keystone CR enqueue reconcile via the owner-ref path (CC-0081)

test_specifications.story: Keystone operator developer retains existing reconcile-trigger behavior
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Existing test continues to pass: a Secret with the staging label but no owner reference and no name match enqueues nothing

test_specifications.story: Reviewer confirms no behavioral regression in end-to-end reconcile triggering
test_specifications.expected: [unit] When a Secret is both name-referenced and owner-referenced to the same Keystone, the mapper returns exactly one reconcile.Request

test_specifications.story: Keystone operator developer retains existing reconcile-trigger behavior
test_specifications.expected: [unit] When two Keystone CRs in the same namespace reference the same Secret name, both are enqueued (one request each)

test_specifications.story: Keystone operator developer finds the indexer setup documented
test_specifications.expected: [unit] The index key is reachable as an exported package-level constant and equals the value used at both register and lookup sites (grep-assertion via reflection or identity check)

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator sees reduced API server load from Secret events
test_specifications.expected: [unit] An interceptor-instrumented client asserts that any c.List call from the mapper carries a non-empty client.MatchingFields option (no unfiltered list)

test_specifications.story: Reviewer confirms no behavioral regression in end-to-end reconcile triggering
test_specifications.expected: [integration] With envtest, register the indexer during controller setup, create a Keystone CR referencing Secret 'keystone-db', then create/update that Secret and assert the Keystone is reconciled (condition change or RequeueAfter observed) within eventuallyTimeout

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator sees reduced API server load from Secret events
test_specifications.expected: [integration] With envtest and a Keystone CR with a known ResourceVersion, create an unrelated Secret in the same namespace and assert the Keystone's status conditions observe no additional reconcile attributable to the Secret event

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator diagnoses a broken indexer without silent reconcile failures
test_specifications.expected: [integration] Injecting a duplicate indexer registration (or a mock that returns an error from IndexField) causes setupEnvTestWithController to fail fast with an error mentioning the index key

review_criteria: BLOCKER: secretToKeystoneMapper no longer issues any unfiltered c.List call; all List calls carry client.MatchingFields{KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey: ...} (REQ-002, REQ-007)
BLOCKER: SetupWithManager returns any error from GetFieldIndexer().IndexField; a unit or integration test covers the failure path (REQ-001)
BLOCKER: All pre-existing TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_* tests pass without changes to their assertions; only the fake client setup (WithIndex) may be adjusted (REQ-004)
CRITICAL: The index key is declared as a single exported constant and referenced at both the IndexField and MatchingFields sites — no literal string duplication (REQ-006)

CRITICAL: Owner-referenced Secrets (rotation staging Secrets from CC-0081) continue to enqueue reconcile via the owner-ref path; no regression in TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_EnqueuesOnStagingSecretUpdate (REQ-003)
CRITICAL: Deduplication by types.NamespacedName is present and covered by TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_DeduplicatesIndexAndOwnerPaths (REQ-005)
CRITICAL: The envtest integration setup (setupEnvTestWithController) registers the indexer via the same helper used in production, not a parallel inline registration (REQ-008)
MINOR: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md is updated with the indexer key, fields, and owner-ref fallback (REQ-006)
MINOR: No new exported API surface beyond KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey and registerSecretNameIndex (avoid scope creep on the public controller package)
MINOR: go vet, golangci-lint, and all unit + integration tests pass
implementation_notes:
ARCHITECTURE (CC-0087)

The change is local to operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go and its tests, plus one docs page and the integration-test setup helper. No API type changes, no CRD changes, no RBAC changes (List on Keystone is already granted; a field indexer uses the cache, not new API verbs).

Key decisions:

1. Single-key indexer (REQ-001, REQ-006). We index the UNION of referenced Secret names under one key, 'spec.secretRefs.name'. A Keystone CR whose two SecretRef fields hold distinct names contributes two index entries; a CR where they are equal contributes one (the extractor deduplicates). An empty string is omitted so missing/optional fields do not pollute the index.

2. Exported const KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey. Tests, docs, and the mapper all reference the same constant. This prevents silent drift between register and lookup sites. The recommended value is 'spec.secretRefs.name'.

3. Split mapper paths (REQ-002, REQ-003). The refactored mapper has two independent enqueue paths:
   a. Referenced-name path: c.List(ctx, &KeystoneList{}, client.InNamespace(obj.GetNamespace()), client.MatchingFields{KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey: obj.GetName()}).
   b. Owner-ref path: iterate obj.GetOwnerReferences(); for each ref whose Kind=="Keystone" and APIVersion==keystonev1alpha1.GroupVersion.String(), enqueue {Namespace: obj.GetNamespace(), Name: ref.Name}. This replaces the old isOwnedBy-scanning-the-list logic with a direct, List-free lookup that preserves CC-0081 behavior for rotation staging Secrets.
   c. Union & deduplicate by types.NamespacedName before returning (REQ-005).

4. Error handling (REQ-002). The indexed List can still fail (cache not synced, context cancelled). We preserve the existing behavior: log via log.FromContext(ctx).Error and return nil — controller-runtime handler.MapFunc contract forbids returning errors.

5. Integration-path parity (REQ-008). setupEnvTestWithController currently wires the reconciler inline with ctrl.NewControllerManagedBy(mgr).For(...).Watches(..., secretToKeystoneMapper(mgr.GetClient())); we add registerSecretNameIndex(ctx, mgr.GetFieldIndexer()) before Complete(r) so the integration test uses the same registration helper as production and fails fast if registration breaks.

6. Fake client test setup. Unit tests that instantiate the mapper must build the fake client with WithIndex(&keystonev1alpha1.Keystone{}, KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey, keystoneSecretNameExtractor) so MatchingFields queries resolve. Use interceptor.Funcs{List: ...} to observe and assert shape of List calls (presence of field selector). controller-runtime's fake client supports both.

ARCHITECTURE DIAGRAM

  Secret event
      |
      v
  secretToKeystoneMapper(ctx, secretObj)
      |
      +-- path A: List Keystones WHERE spec.secretRefs.name == secretObj.Name (namespaced)
      |       -> append {Namespace, Name} for each match
      |
      +-- path B: for each ownerRef in secretObj.OwnerReferences
      |       if Kind=="Keystone" and APIVersion matches
      |          -> append {Namespace: secretObj.Namespace, Name: ownerRef.Name}
      |
      v
  dedupe by types.NamespacedName -> []reconcile.Request

DATA FLOW

  At manager startup:
    SetupWithManager(mgr) -> registerSecretNameIndex(ctx, mgr.GetFieldIndexer())
        -> mgr.GetFieldIndexer().IndexField(ctx, &Keystone{}, KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey, extractor)
    Cache informer populates the index from Keystone objects.

  At steady state:
    kube-apiserver -> informer -> Secret event -> mapper -> cache lookup (no apiserver round-trip) -> reconcile.Request

PITFALLS

  * Forgetting to register the index in envtest tests -> MatchingFields returns no hits; add the registration to the shared helper (task 3.1).
  * APIVersion check on ownerRef. keystonev1alpha1.GroupVersion.String() is authoritative; avoid hardcoding 'keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/v1alpha1' as a string (REQ-003).
  * Owner-ref path must NOT dereference a Keystone by Get; that would add a round-trip per Secret event and defeat the purpose. Just construct the NamespacedName.
  * The old isOwnedBy helper becomes unused if not called elsewhere; grep first and delete if orphaned. If still referenced, leave it.
  * Empty-string extraction: if extractor returns [""], controller-runtime will index under the empty key; ensure the filter drops empties (REQ-001).
  * handler.MapFunc contract: never return an error; log and return nil on List failure (REQ-002).

FILES TO MODIFY

  operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go          (SetupWithManager, secretToKeystoneMapper, new extractor + const + helper)
  operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go     (new + updated unit tests)
  operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go             (setupEnvTestWithController + three new tests)
  docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md                                  (document indexer)

FILES TO READ FOR CONTEXT (do not modify)

  operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go                      (SecretRefSpec fields)
  operators/keystone/main.go                                             (controller wiring unchanged)
  internal/common/testutil/envtest/setup.go                              (envtest entry points)
